title: Jersey Opera House gets third chairperson of 2025
content:
Jersey Opera House has appointed a new interim chairperson, in the latest chapter of a story which has seen multiple resignations since the start of 2025.

Laura Robertson, a lawyer, has stepped into the position having already served as part of the board of directors.

The decision comes after Cyril Whelan announcedon Wednesdayhe was to resign having only just succeeded another outgoing interim chairperson in April.

Jersey Opera House's Grade II listed building in St Helierreopened after five years in Mayfollowing a major £13m refurbishment scheme.

A spokesperson for the Board of Jersey Opera House said the announcement was part of an "exciting new chapter".

They added: "All the board bring a shared vision and a deep passion for the long-term success of the Opera House, which is widely regarded as the crown jewel of Jersey's cultural landscape.

"The Board and Executive are committed to delivering a diverse, inclusive programme, unlocking renewed opportunities for performers and audiences alike.

"This journey is grounded in creativity, collaboration, and community."
